evals.name: slow-raycaster
evals.notes: Both variants correctly diagnose rl.DrawPixel as the bottleneck and recommend the rl.Image framebuffer pattern with a complete main-loop. The pattern is well-represented in raylib documentation and training data.

evals.name: read-png-pixels
missed_by_baseline: image-can-unload-early: baseline used direct img.data cast (correct alternative path) and warned against unloading early — but for the LoadImageColors path the data IS copied. Baseline didn't address this specific lifecycle detail.
evals.notes: Baseline took a different (also correct) approach using rl.ImageFormat + casting img.data to [^]rl.Color directly. This avoids the LoadImageColors copy but binds the pixel array's lifetime to img. The skill version follows the LoadImageColors path that's idiomatic for read-only sampling.

analysis: Modest lift on eval 2 — baseline diverged to a different correct pattern, missing the specific copy-semantics nuance the skill teaches. Eval 1 (framebuffer pattern) shows no lift; the pattern is well-documented in raylib materials and training data. The skill's value here is in surfacing one specific raylib API path (LoadImageColors → [^]rl.Color → UnloadImageColors) over a competing one (img.data cast).
